name: CIMA PR09: Alto do Trevim - Lousã
name_original: Salita a Trevim da Lousã – Giro ad anello
region: Lousã e Vilarinho e Vilarinho e Vilarinho
description: This difficult 33.5-mile road cycling route to Alto do Trevim offers significant elevation gain and panoramic views in Serra da Lousã.
region_intro: Road cycling around Lousã e Vilarinho offers diverse landscapes and challenging terrains within the Coimbra District of Portugal. The region is dominated by the Serra da Lousã mountains, providing significant elevation gains and varied routes. Cyclists will find mostly well-paved surfaces, with some segments potentially unpaved. This area is recognized for its natural beauty and suitability for diverse cycling experiences. Best road cycling routes in Lousã e Vilarinho The most popular road cycling route is CIMA PR09: Alto do Trevim - Lousã, a 33.5 miles (53.9 km) trail that takes 3 hours 19 minutes to complete. This difficult route connects Lousã village to Alto do Trevim, the highest point in Serra da Lousã. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Talasnal Schist Village – Candal Schist Village loop from Lousã, a difficult 62.3 miles (100.2 km) path. This route winds through picturesque Aldeias do Xisto (Schist Villages), offering a glimpse into traditional Portuguese life. Local road cyclists also love the Loureiro Picnic Park – Palheiros River Beach loop from Lousã, a 43.6 miles (70.2 km) trail leading through river valleys and natural pools, often completed in about 3 hours 20 minutes. Road cycling in Lousã e Vilarinho is defined by the Serra da Lousã mountains, ancient Aldeias do Xisto, and scenic river valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Lousã e Vilarinho are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from more than 40 reviews. More than 1000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Lousã e Vilarinho's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in the Lousã e Vilarinho region?
A: The Lousã e Vilarinho region offers a wide variety of road cycling routes. There are over 45 routes available, catering to different skill levels, from moderate to difficult.

Q: What kind of terrain and difficulty can I expect on road cycling routes in Lousã e Vilarinho?
A: Road cycling in Lousã e Vilarinho is characterized by the challenging terrain of the Serra da Lousã mountains, featuring significant elevation gains. Routes range from moderate to difficult, with mostly well-paved surfaces, though some segments might be unpaved. You'll encounter climbs like the one to Alto do Trevim, which can involve around 1,000 meters of climbing over 20 km.

Q: What natural features and landmarks can I explore while road cycling in Lousã e Vilarinho?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty and historical sites. You can cycle past the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/769185" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senhora da Piedade River Pool&lt;/a&gt;, explore the picturesque Aldeias do Xisto (Schist Villages) like Talasnal and Candal, or visit the historic Lousã Castle. Routes also offer breathtaking viewpoints across the Serra da Lousã and opportunities to see waterfalls such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2890426" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pedra Ferida Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available in Lousã e Vilarinho?
A: Yes, many routes in the region are designed as loops. For instance, you can enjoy the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20134163" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Talasnal Schist Village – Candal Schist Village loop from Lousã&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 100 km and passes through several traditional villages. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32164153" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Loureiro Picnic Park – Palheiros River Beach loop from Lousã&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 70 km ride through river valleys.

Q: Are there any particularly challenging climbs for experienced road cyclists?
A: Absolutely. The region is known for its demanding climbs. A prime example is the ascent to Alto do Trevim, the highest point in Serra da Lousã at 1,202 meters. The route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2314154014/cima-pr09-alto-do-trevim-lousa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;CIMA PR09: Alto do Trevim - Lousã&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 53.9 km ride with over 1,400 meters of elevation gain, offering panoramic views as a reward.

Q: Can I cycle through the famous Schist Villages in Lousã e Vilarinho?
A: Yes, the Aldeias do Xisto (Schist Villages) are a highlight of the region and accessible by road bike. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20134163" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Talasnal Schist Village – Candal Schist Village loop from Lousã&lt;/a&gt; allow you to experience these ancient stone hamlets, including Talasnal, Candal, and others, offering a unique cultural and scenic experience.

Q: Are there routes that pass by river beaches or waterfalls where I can cool off?
A: Yes, several routes incorporate the region's beautiful river beaches and natural pools. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32164153" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Loureiro Picnic Park – Palheiros River Beach loop from Lousã&lt;/a&gt; is a great option. You can also find spots like Praia Fluvial da Senhora da Piedade, known for its crystal-clear pools, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4006009" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ribeira de Espinho Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt;, perfect for a refreshing stop.

Q: When is the best time of year for road cycling in Lousã e Vilarinho?
A: The region generally offers good conditions for road cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn typically provide pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes, making them ideal. Summer can be warm, but the presence of river beaches and shaded forest sections can offer relief. Winter rides are possible, but check local weather conditions for potential rain or colder temperatures at higher elevations.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Lousã e Vilarinho?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 40 reviews. Cyclists frequently praise the diverse landscapes, the challenging climbs, and the unique experience of riding through the historic Schist Villages. The combination of natural beauty and cultural heritage makes it a memorable destination.

Q: Are there any longer, multi-day road cycling options in the area?
A: While many routes are designed for day trips, the extensive network and the region's connection to the iconic National Road 2 (EN2) allow for longer, multi-day explorations. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32206277" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Penela Castle – Castelo de Penela loop from Lousã&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 138.8 km route that could be part of a larger tour, connecting various points of interest and offering significant elevation.

Q: Can I find routes that are less strenuous for a more relaxed ride?
A: While the region is known for its challenging terrain, there are also moderate options available. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32164153" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Loureiro Picnic Park – Palheiros River Beach loop from Lousã&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 70.2 km route with less intense elevation gain compared to the mountain climbs, allowing for a more relaxed pace while still enjoying the scenic river valleys.
tips:
Candal is part of the so-called "Serra da Lousã", an area characterized by lush nature and breathtaking landscapes. 

This village is a typical example of the "aldeias de xisto" (schist villages), built entirely with local stone, which integrate harmoniously into the surrounding environment. Candal offers an atmosphere of peace and tranquility, with its traditional houses, waterways and paths surrounded by vegetation.
The highest point of Serra da Lousã

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the CIMA PR09: Alto do Trevim - Lousã cycling route?
faqs.answer: This challenging racebike route, covering approximately 53.9 km with over 1400 meters of elevation gain, typically takes around 3 hours and 20 minutes to complete for an experienced cyclist. However, your actual time may vary depending on your fitness level and how often you stop to enjoy the views.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the climb to Alto do Trevim?
faqs.answer: The climb from Lousã to Alto do Trevim is considered a difficult, Category 1 ascent, making it an 'Epic Climb' for road cyclists. It features a substantial vertical gain of nearly 1200 meters over 25-26 km, with an average gradient of 4-4.7%. Be prepared for steeper sections, especially in the final 2 kilometers, where inclines can reach 6-7.9%, with some passages as steep as 10-11%.

faqs.question: Where does the CIMA PR09: Alto do Trevim - Lousã route start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The route typically starts in Lousã. While specific trailhead parking isn't detailed, Lousã is a town with various parking options. It's advisable to check local parking availability in Lousã before your ride.

faqs.question: Are there any public transport options to reach the starting point in Lousã?
faqs.answer: Lousã is accessible by public transport, primarily buses, from larger cities in Portugal. However, transporting a road bike on public transport can sometimes be challenging, so it's best to check with the specific bus operators regarding their policies for bicycles.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain and road conditions can I expect on the CIMA PR09 route?
faqs.answer: The route follows the N236 road, which can have worn surfaces in places due to harsh winters. As you ascend through the Serra da Lousã, the vegetation becomes scarcer, exposing riders to wind, particularly near the summit. The road is paved, but its condition requires attention, especially on descents.

faqs.question: What are the main viewpoints or landmarks along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers stunning scenery throughout. Key highlights include the charming settlement of Candal. The summit, Alto do Trevim, provides spectacular panoramic views, and the Tarrasteira Viewpoint is another excellent spot. Don't miss the popular 'Baloiço do Trevim' (Trevim Swing) at the summit for a unique photo opportunity with breathtaking backdrops.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to cycle the CIMA PR09: Alto do Trevim - Lousã route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route is generally during the spring (April-June) and early autumn (September-October) when temperatures are milder and the weather is more stable. Summer can be very hot, and winter may bring harsh conditions, including worn road surfaces and strong winds at higher elevations.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the CIMA PR09 cycling route?
faqs.answer: As this is primarily a road cycling route, dogs are generally not restricted on public roads. However, if you plan to stop at any specific points of interest or natural parks, it's always best to check local regulations regarding pets. Always ensure your dog is safe and does not interfere with traffic if you bring them along.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for cycling in the Serra da Lousã?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for cycling the CIMA PR09 route or generally within the Serra da Lousã. It's a public road, and access is free. However, always respect local regulations and private property.

faqs.question: Does the CIMA PR09 route intersect with other well-known trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, while the CIMA PR09: Alto do Trevim * Lousa route covers the majority of this tour, sections of the route also intersect with or run alongside other trails such as Rota da Levada, Rota das Aldeias do Xisto da Lousã, and Rota do Trevim.

faqs.question: What makes the Alto do Trevim climb a rewarding experience for cyclists?
faqs.answer: Beyond the significant physical challenge, the Alto do Trevim climb offers immense rewards. It's considered one of the classic 'Epic Climbs' of Central Portugal, leading to one of the highest points in the Serra da Lousã. The panoramic 360-degree views from the summit, stretching to the Serra da Estrela, the Atlantic Ocean, and even Marvão, make the demanding ascent truly worthwhile.
